_global_instructions: Welcome! You can update the text inside the quotes below to change the site content. IMPORTANT: Only change the values on the right side of the colon (:). Ensure you don't delete any quotation marks.
_help_features: Change these to true or false (without quotes) to instantly show or hide sections on the live site.
_help_global: General settings that apply across the entire site.
_help_navigation: The links that appear in the top navigation bar. If a feature (like showSchedule) is set to false, its link will automatically hide.
_help_hero: This is the very first introductory section people see at the top of the website.

hero.title: IEEE CIS Kerala Section
hero.subtitle: Summer Internship 2025
hero.description: A Premier Learning Experience in Computational Intelligence

_help_about: The section answering basic questions about the internship structure.

about.title: About the Internship
about.description: The IEEE CIS Kerala Section Summer Internship Program offers a unique opportunity for students to gain hands-on experience in cutting-edge computational intelligence technologies under the guidance of expert mentors.
whyApplyItems: Hands-on experience with cutting-edge AI technologies
Mentorship from leading experts in the field
Build your professional network in the industry
Work on real-world projects with practical applications
Enhance your resume with IEEE certification

_help_topics: The list of topics covered during the internship. Ensure the 'icon' name is one of the supported React Icons.

topics.title: Topics Covered
topics.description: Explore cutting-edge technologies and domains in computational intelligence through our comprehensive curriculum.

items.title: Machine Learning
items.description: Fundamentals of ML algorithms, supervised and unsupervised learning techniques, and practical applications.

items.title: Artificial Intelligence
items.description: Core AI concepts, problem-solving techniques, and modern AI systems development.

items.title: Edge AI
items.description: Deploy AI models on edge devices, optimize for resource constraints, and real-time applications.

items.title: Natural Language Processing
items.description: Text processing, sentiment analysis, language models, and conversational AI systems.

items.title: Internet of Things
items.description: IoT architecture, sensor networks, data collection, and intelligent IoT applications.

items.title: Cybersecurity
items.description: Security for AI systems, adversarial machine learning, and ethical hacking techniques.

items.title: Data Analytics
items.description: Data visualization, statistical analysis, and business intelligence applications.

items.title: Big Data
items.description: Process and analyze large datasets using distributed computing frameworks.

items.title: Computational Biology
items.description: AI applications in genomics, protein structure prediction, and drug discovery.

_help_eligibility: Guidelines on who can apply. To add a new list item, copy an existing entry including the quotes, and ensure there's a comma between elements.

eligibility.title: Eligibility & Selection
eligibility.description: Our merit-based selection process ensures we identify the most promising candidates.
criteria: Currently enrolled in B.Tech/BE/M.Tech/ME in Computer Science, Electronics, or related disciplines
Strong foundation in mathematics, programming, and basic machine learning concepts
Proficiency in Python or another programming language relevant to AI/ML
Proven interest in computational intelligence through projects or coursework
ineligibleFactors: Students in their final semester (due to program duration requirements)
Non-technical background without sufficient programming experience
Unable to commit at least 20 hours per week during the program duration

selectionProcess.title: Application Review
selectionProcess.desc: All applications are reviewed based on academic background, relevant experience, and statement of purpose.

selectionProcess.title: Technical Assessment
selectionProcess.desc: Shortlisted candidates may be asked to complete a brief technical assessment or project.

selectionProcess.title: Final Selection
selectionProcess.desc: Selected candidates will be notified via email with further instructions for enrollment.

_help_schedule: List of dates and events. You can change 'isActive' to true whenever an event completes.

schedule.title: Schedule
schedule.description: Important dates for the internship program.

timeline.title: Applications Open
timeline.description: Online applications for the summer internship begin through the IEEE portal.

timeline.title: Application Deadline
timeline.description: Last date to submit your application and supporting documents.

timeline.title: Shortlist Announcement
timeline.description: List of shortlisted candidates will be published on the website.

timeline.title: Email Notification to Selected Students
timeline.description: Emails have been sent to selected students with further instructions.

timeline.title: Registration Deadline
timeline.description: Selected candidates must pay the registration fee and submit their details by 10:00 PM.

timeline.title: Orientation Program
timeline.description: Introductory session to welcome participants and explain the internship structure.

timeline.title: Internship Period
timeline.description: 10-week hands-on internship across various host institutions.

timeline.title: Project Presentations
timeline.description: Final presentations and evaluations by mentors and faculty.

timeline.title: Certification & Closing
timeline.description: Closing ceremony and distribution of certificates to successful candidates.

_help_registration: Payment portal texts and the full array of selected candidates. Use **text** to make a word bold.

registration.title: Payment & Confirmation Instructions
registration.description: Congratulations to all selected candidates! Please check your inbox for the registration email. Complete your registration by **May 16th, 10:00 PM** by submitting the required details and uploading your payment receipt.
registration.warningNote: Failure to complete registration before the deadline will result in forfeiting your selection.
registration.formInstruction: Please use the Google Form below to find the payment details and submit your proof of payment.

_help_mentorAllocation: Contains the structured guidelines layout and the final list mapping students to Mentors. Set isList to true if you are defining an array for 'listItems'. Use **text** to embed bold words inside normal content strings.

guidelines.title: 1. Duration & Mode
guidelines.content:
**Internship Period:** Any 1 month between May 20 and July 31, 2025
**Mode:** Hybrid

guidelines.title: 2. Student Assignment
guidelines.content: Students are assigned based on mutual academic interests and core CIS themes. The list of students allotted for each faculty is available below. Students have been instructed to reach out to their mentors via email.

guidelines.title: 3. Themes to Guide
listItems: Neural Networks (incl. deep learning, CNNs, RNNs, etc.)

guidelines.title: 4. Expectations from Mentors
listItems: Provide an appropriate problem statement or project area within your expertise
Hold regular check-ins with your mentees (weekly is recommended)
Offer feedback on project progress and direction
Verify the student’s final submission (report and presentation)

guidelines.title: 5. Deliverables from Students
listItems: Final internship report (10–15 pages) — template to be shared separately

guidelines.title: 6. Certification
guidelines.content: Students will receive a certificate jointly from the IEEE CIS Joint Chapter and IEEE Kerala Section after the completion and verification of the internship.
